notable_excerpts: Our people remain central to our sustained success. We continue to invest in nurturing a supportive, inclusive and empowering workplace - one that fosters innovation, collaboration and a strong sense of belonging.
Named among the Best Companies for Women in India 2024 (BFSI)
Honoured as an Exemplar of Inclusion in the Most Inclusive Companies Index 2025, by Avtar & Seramount - for the second consecutive year
We plan to raise up to ₹750 crore in subordinated debt in one or more tranches in H2. The fund raise is expected to enhance solvency by around 7%.
The solvency ratio was at 175%, reflecting a combination of dividend payout, repayment of ₹600 crore subordinated debt in Q2, writing more protection business and the GST impact.

chain_of_thought: The earnings call transcript for HDFC Life lacked any significant environmental disclosures, resulting in a score of 0 for the environmental pillar. The social pillar was well-covered, with a focus on employee-centric practices and diversity, earning a score of 75. Governance disclosures were moderate, focusing on financial stability and risk management, leading to a score of 65. The overall ESG score is calculated based on the weighted contributions of each pillar.
strengths: Strong focus on employee-centric practices and diversity.
Clear communication on financial stability and risk management.
